project.name: DocSpec Annotations (Java)
project.description: Annotation library for DocSpec universal documentation specification

members.name: ContextInput

methods.name: name
methods.description: Name of the input parameter.

returns.description: the input name, must not be empty

methods.name: source
methods.description:
Origin or source of the input value, such as {@code "environment"},
 {@code "request.body"}, or {@code "config"}.

methods.name: description
methods.description: Prose description of the input's purpose and expected format.

methods.name: items
methods.description:
Enumeration of specific items, keys, or variants that this input
 accepts.

members.name: ContextUses

methods.name: artifact
methods.description: Maven-style coordinates or identifier of the referenced artifact.

returns.description: the artifact identifier, must not be empty

methods.name: what
methods.description:
Name of the specific component, class, or resource being used from
 the artifact.

returns.description: the component name, must not be empty

methods.name: why
methods.description: Prose explanation of why this dependency exists.

members.name: DocAsyncAPI

methods.name: channel
methods.description: The AsyncAPI channel name.

methods.name: operation
methods.description:
The operation type on this channel.

Common values are {@code "publish"} and {@code "subscribe"}.

returns.description: the operation type, or empty if not specified

members.name: DocAudience
members.description:
Declares the intended audience visibility level for the annotated element.

Documentation processors use this annotation to partition API documentation into tiers. Supported values are:

  • {@code "public"} — visible to all external consumers
  • {@code "partner"} — visible only to partner integrations
  • {@code "internal"} — visible only within the organization
{@code
 @DocAudience("partner")
 public class PartnerOnboardingService { ... }
 }

methods.name: value
methods.description:
The audience level for this element.

Must be one of {@code "public"}, {@code "partner"}, or {@code "internal"}.

members.name: DocBoundary

methods.name: value
methods.description:
A description of the architectural boundary.

Common values include {@code "anti-corruption layer"}, {@code "adapter"}, {@code "facade"}, and {@code "gateway"}.

members.name: DocCommand

methods.name: value
methods.description: The command name.

methods.name: aggregate
methods.description: The target aggregate that this command modifies.

returns.description: the aggregate name, or empty if not applicable

members.name: DocCommutative

methods.name: value
methods.description: Optional description of the commutative property.

members.name: DocCompare

methods.name: value
methods.description:
A description of the comparison semantics.

Common values include {@code "natural ordering"}, {@code "by-name"}, {@code "by-date"}, and {@code "case-insensitive"}.

members.name: DocConservation

methods.name: value
methods.description: A description of the conservation law that holds for this method.

members.name: DocContext

methods.name: id
methods.description: Unique identifier for this context.

methods.name: name
methods.description: Human-readable display name for the context.

methods.name: inputs
methods.description: Runtime inputs required by this context.

methods.name: flow
methods.description: Identifier of the flow associated with this context.

returns.description: the flow id, or empty if not associated with a specific flow

methods.name: uses
methods.description: Cross-project dependencies consumed by this context.

members.name: DocDeterministic

methods.name: value
methods.description: Optional description of what makes this method deterministic.

members.name: DocEndpoint

methods.name: value
methods.description:
The HTTP method and path of the API endpoint, formatted as {@code "METHOD /path"}.

Examples: {@code "GET /v1/curricula"}, {@code "POST /v1/curricula/generate"}, {@code "DELETE /v1/curricula/{id}"}.

returns.description: the endpoint descriptor, must not be empty

members.name: DocError
members.description:
Documents an error code on the annotated type, providing structured information for API consumers to understand and handle failures.

Each annotated type represents a distinct error condition with its own code, HTTP status, description, possible causes, and resolution guidance.

{@code
 @DocError(
     code = "CURR_001",
     httpStatus = 422,
     description = "The job description is too short to generate a curriculum.",
     causes = {"Input text has fewer than 50 characters", "Input text is blank after trimming"},
     resolution = "Provide a job description with at least 50 characters.",
     since = "1.0"
 )
 public class JobDescriptionTooShortException extends ApiException { ... }
 }

methods.name: code
methods.description: Application-specific error code.

returns.description: the error code, must not be empty

methods.name: httpStatus
methods.description:
HTTP status code associated with this error.

A value of {@code -1} (the default) indicates that no specific HTTP status is associated.

returns.description: the HTTP status code, or {@code -1} if not applicable

methods.name: description
methods.description: Human-readable description of the error condition.

methods.name: causes
methods.description: Possible root causes that may lead to this error.

methods.name: resolution
methods.description: Guidance for the API consumer on how to resolve or avoid this error.

methods.name: since
methods.description: Version in which this error code was first introduced.

members.name: DocEvent
members.description:
Documents a webhook or event that the annotated type or method produces or handles.

This annotation captures the full contract of an event, including its delivery semantics, retry behavior, and the conditions under which it fires.

{@code
 @DocEvent(
     name = "curriculum.generated",
     description = "Fired when a curriculum has been successfully generated.",
     trigger = "Successful completion of the generation flow",
     channel = "webhooks",
     deliveryGuarantee = "at-least-once",
     retryPolicy = "Exponential backoff, max 5 retries over 24 hours",
     since = "1.0"
 )
 public class CurriculumGeneratedEvent { ... }
 }

methods.name: name
methods.description: Canonical name of the event, typically in dot-separated format.

returns.description: the event name, must not be empty

methods.name: description
methods.description: Human-readable description of what this event represents.

methods.name: trigger
methods.description: Description of the condition or action that causes this event to fire.

methods.name: channel
methods.description:
The delivery channel or transport mechanism for this event, such as
 {@code "webhooks"}, {@code "sns"}, or {@code "kafka"}.

methods.name: deliveryGuarantee
methods.description:
Delivery guarantee semantics, such as {@code "at-most-once"},
 {@code "at-least-once"}, or {@code "exactly-once"}.

methods.name: retryPolicy
methods.description: Description of the retry policy applied when delivery fails.

methods.name: since
methods.description: Version in which this event was first introduced.

members.name: DocExample

methods.name: title
methods.description: Display title for the example.

methods.name: language
methods.description: Programming language of the example code, used for syntax highlighting.

methods.name: code
methods.description:
Inline source code for the example.

Mutually exclusive with #file() in practice; if both are provided, processor behavior is implementation-defined.

methods.name: file
methods.description:
Path to an external file containing the example source code.

The path is resolved relative to the project root by documentation processors.

members.name: DocExamples
members.description:
Container annotation for repeatable DocExample annotations.

This annotation is not intended to be used directly. When multiple DocExample annotations are placed on the same element, the compiler automatically wraps them in a {@code DocExamples} container.

methods.name: value
methods.description: The contained DocExample annotations.

members.name: DocField

methods.name: description
methods.description: Prose description of the field's purpose.

methods.name: since
methods.description: Version in which this field was first introduced.

members.name: DocFlow

methods.name: id
methods.description: Unique identifier for this flow.

returns.description: the flow identifier, must not be empty

methods.name: name
methods.description: Human-readable display name for the flow.

returns.description: the flow name, or empty string to derive from #id()

methods.name: description
methods.description: Prose description of what the flow accomplishes.

methods.name: trigger
methods.description: Description of the event or condition that initiates this flow.

methods.name: steps
methods.description: Ordered sequence of steps that compose this flow.

members.name: DocGraphQL

methods.name: type
methods.description:
The GraphQL type.

Common values are {@code "Query"}, {@code "Mutation"}, and {@code "Subscription"}.

returns.description: the GraphQL type, or empty if inferred from context

methods.name: field
methods.description: The GraphQL field name.

returns.description: the field name, or empty to infer from the method name

members.name: DocGRPC

methods.name: service
methods.description: The gRPC service name.

returns.description: the service name, or empty to infer from the class name

methods.name: method
methods.description: The gRPC method name.

returns.description: the method name, or empty to infer from the Java method name

members.name: DocHidden
DocIdempotent

methods.name: value
methods.description: Optional description of the idempotency guarantee.

members.name: DocIntentional

methods.name: value
methods.description: A description of the method's primary intent.

methods.name: preserves
methods.description:
Fields or properties that must be preserved (unchanged) through the
 method's execution.

returns.description: an array of field or property names that are preserved

members.name: DocInvariant

methods.name: on
methods.description:
The entity or class the invariant applies to.

If empty, the invariant applies to the annotated type itself.

returns.description: the target entity name, or empty to use the annotated type

methods.name: rules
methods.description: The invariant rules expressed as human-readable expressions.

returns.description: an array of invariant rule expressions

members.name: DocMethod

methods.name: value
methods.description:
Description of what this method does. This replaces the first line
 of a JavaDoc comment.

methods.name: returns
methods.description: Description of the return value. Replaces {@code @return} in JavaDoc.

methods.name: params
methods.description: Structured parameter documentation. Replaces {@code @param} tags in JavaDoc.

methods.name: throwsDoc
methods.description: Structured throws documentation. Replaces {@code @throws} tags in JavaDoc.

methods.name: since
methods.description: Version in which this method was first introduced.

methods.name: deprecated
methods.description: Deprecation notice. Empty means not deprecated.

methods.name: example
methods.description: Code example showing usage of this method.

members.name: DocModule
members.description:
Groups annotated types or packages into a logical documentation module.

A module represents a cohesive unit of functionality within the API surface. Every type or package belonging to the same module should share the same #id(), allowing documentation tooling to aggregate and present them together.

{@code
 @DocModule(
     id = "curricula",
     name = "Curricula Management",
     description = "APIs for creating, updating, and querying curricula.",
     since = "1.0",
     audience = "public"
 )
 public class CurriculaService { ... }
 }

methods.name: id
methods.description:
Unique identifier for this module. Used to group related types across
 packages.

returns.description: the module identifier, must not be empty

methods.name: name
methods.description: Human-readable display name for the module.

returns.description: the module name, or empty string to derive from #id()

methods.name: description
methods.description: Prose description of the module's purpose and scope.

methods.name: since
methods.description: Version in which this module was first introduced.

methods.name: audience
methods.description:
Intended audience for this module's documentation.

Common values are {@code "public"}, {@code "partner"}, and {@code "internal"}.

members.name: DocMonotonic

methods.name: direction
methods.description:
The direction of monotonic change.

Valid values are {@code "increasing"} and {@code "decreasing"}.

members.name: DocOptional
DocOrdering

methods.name: value
methods.description: A description of the ordering guarantee.

members.name: DocPerformance

methods.name: expectedLatency
methods.description: The expected latency for this method.

returns.description: a latency description, e.g. {@code "< 6s at p99"}

methods.name: bottleneck
methods.description: A known performance bottleneck in this method.

returns.description: a description of the bottleneck, or empty if none known

members.name: DocPII

methods.name: value
methods.description:
The type of PII contained in this field.

Common values include {@code "email"}, {@code "phone"}, {@code "name"}, {@code "address"}, {@code "ssn"}, {@code "dob"}, {@code "ip"}, {@code "financial"}, {@code "health"}, {@code "biometric"}, and {@code "other"}.

methods.name: retention
methods.description: The retention policy governing how long this data may be stored.

returns.description: the retention policy description, e.g. {@code "90 days"}

methods.name: gdprBasis
methods.description:
The GDPR legal basis for processing this personal data.

Common values include {@code "consent"}, {@code "contract"}, {@code "legal-obligation"}, {@code "vital-interest"}, {@code "public-task"}, and {@code "legitimate-interest"}.

returns.description: the GDPR legal basis, or empty if not applicable

methods.name: encrypted
methods.description: Whether this field is stored in encrypted form.

returns.description: {@code true} if the field value is encrypted at rest

methods.name: neverLog
methods.description: Whether this field must never appear in log output.

returns.description: {@code true} if the field must be excluded from all logging

methods.name: neverReturn
methods.description: Whether this field must never be returned in API responses.

returns.description: {@code true} if the field must be excluded from all responses

members.name: DocPreserves

methods.name: fields
methods.description:
The names of fields or properties that must remain unchanged after
 the method executes.

returns.description: an array of field or property names

members.name: DocRelation

methods.name: value
methods.description:
The type of relationship.

Common values include {@code "parent-child"}, {@code "peer"}, {@code "depends-on"}, {@code "owns"}, and {@code "references"}.

methods.name: target
methods.description: The target entity of this relationship.

returns.description: the target entity name, or empty to infer from the field type

members.name: DocSensitive

methods.name: reason
methods.description: The reason this data is considered sensitive.

returns.description: a human-readable explanation of why this data is sensitive

members.name: DocSpecExample

methods.name: attachTo
methods.description:
Fully qualified reference to the documented element that this example should be attached to.

The format is typically {@code "fully.qualified.ClassName#methodName"} or {@code "fully.qualified.ClassName"} for type-level examples.

returns.description: the target element reference, must not be empty

methods.name: title
methods.description: Display title for the example.

members.name: DocStateMachine

methods.name: states
methods.description: The set of valid states in the state machine.

methods.name: initial
methods.description: The initial state when the state machine is created.

returns.description: the initial state name, or empty if unspecified

methods.name: transitions
methods.description: The allowed transitions expressed in {@code "FROM -> TO"} format.

members.name: DocTags

methods.name: value
methods.description: One or more tags to associate with the annotated type.

returns.description: the array of tag strings, must contain at least one element

members.name: DocTestSkip

methods.name: reason
methods.description:
The reason why automated test generation should be skipped for this
 method.

members.name: DocTestStrategy
members.description:
Documents the test strategy for the annotated type or method.

This annotation captures how a component should be tested, including the testing level and key scenarios. Documentation tooling can use this to generate test coverage reports and identify gaps.

{@code
 @DocTestStrategy(value = "integration",
                  scenarios = {"happy path with valid input",
                               "invalid input returns 400",
                               "concurrent access is safe"})
 public class PaymentService { ... }
 }

methods.name: value
methods.description:
The testing strategy or level.

Common values include {@code "unit"}, {@code "integration"}, {@code "e2e"}, and {@code "property-based"}.

methods.name: scenarios
methods.description: Key test scenarios that should be covered.

members.name: DocUses
members.description:
Declares a cross-project or cross-module reference from the annotated element to an artifact, flow, step, or member in another project.

This annotation is Repeatable, allowing multiple cross-project references on the same element. Documentation tooling can use these references to build dependency graphs and cross-link documentation across repositories.

{@code
 @DocUses(
     artifact = "com.example:auth-service",
     flow = "token-validation",
     step = "verify",
     description = "Validates the bearer token before processing the request."
 )
 public Response handleRequest(Request request) { ... }
 }

methods.name: artifact
methods.description: Maven-style coordinates or identifier of the referenced artifact.

returns.description: the artifact identifier, must not be empty

methods.name: flow
methods.description: Identifier of the referenced flow within the artifact.

returns.description: the flow id, or empty if not referencing a specific flow

methods.name: step
methods.description: Identifier of the referenced step within the flow.

returns.description: the step id, or empty if not referencing a specific step

methods.name: member
methods.description: Name of the referenced member (method, field, class) within the artifact.

returns.description: the member name, or empty if not referencing a specific member

methods.name: description
methods.description:
Prose description of why this reference exists and how the dependency
 is used.

members.name: DocUsesAll
members.description:
Container annotation for repeatable DocUses annotations.

This annotation is not intended to be used directly. When multiple DocUses annotations are placed on the same element, the compiler automatically wraps them in a {@code DocUsesAll} container.

methods.name: value
methods.description: The contained DocUses annotations.

members.name: DocWebSocket

methods.name: path
methods.description: The WebSocket endpoint path.

methods.name: messages
methods.description: The message types handled by this WebSocket endpoint.

returns.description: an array of message type names

members.name: Param

methods.name: name
methods.description: Parameter name — must match the actual Java parameter name.

methods.name: value
methods.description: Description of the parameter.

members.name: Step
members.description:
Describes a single step within a DocFlow.

This annotation has an empty Target and is designed exclusively for use as a nested annotation inside DocFlow#steps().

Supported #type() values are:

  • {@code "process"} — general processing step (default)
  • {@code "ai"} — step involving an AI/LLM call
  • {@code "storage"} — data persistence step
  • {@code "trigger"} — event trigger step
  • {@code "retry"} — retry/compensation step
  • {@code "external"} — call to an external system
  • {@code "bridge"} — integration bridge between systems
  • {@code "observability"} — logging, metrics, or tracing step
{@code
 @Step(
     id = "generate",
     name = "Generate via LLM",
     actor = "OpenAI",
     actorQualified = "com.openai.Client",
     description = "Sends the parsed input to the LLM for curriculum generation.",
     type = "ai",
     ai = true,
     inputs = {"parsedInput"},
     outputs = {"rawCurriculum"}
 )
 }

methods.name: id
methods.description: Unique identifier for this step within its flow.

returns.description: the step identifier, must not be empty

methods.name: name
methods.description: Human-readable display name for the step.

returns.description: the step name, or empty string to derive from #id()

methods.name: actor
methods.description:
Short name of the actor (system, service, or role) responsible for
 executing this step.

methods.name: actorQualified
methods.description: Fully qualified name of the actor, such as a class or service identifier.

methods.name: description
methods.description: Prose description of what this step does.

methods.name: type
methods.description:
The type of this step, indicating its role in the flow.

Must be one of: {@code "process"}, {@code "ai"}, {@code "storage"}, {@code "trigger"}, {@code "retry"}, {@code "external"}, {@code "bridge"}, or {@code "observability"}.

methods.name: ai
methods.description: Whether this step involves an AI or LLM invocation.

returns.description: {@code true} if the step uses AI, {@code false} otherwise

methods.name: retryTarget
methods.description: Identifier of the step to retry or fall back to on failure.

returns.description: the retry target step id, or empty if no retry is configured

methods.name: inputs
methods.description: Names or identifiers of the data inputs consumed by this step.

methods.name: outputs
methods.description: Names or identifiers of the data outputs produced by this step.

members.name: ThrowsDoc

methods.name: type
methods.description: Fully qualified or simple name of the exception type.

methods.name: value
methods.description: Description of when this exception is thrown.
